reasoning: The resolution threshold is established by the final close price before Mar 30, which is 20.55 on Mar 28. The stock experienced a sharp drop from 21.75 on Mar 20 to 20.55. Our statistical yfinance trend model estimates a bounce back, predicting a mean edge of 20.72 at April 6 (vs the reference 20.55), indicating downward momentum may reverse or mean-revert in the extreme short term. Given standard GBM drift principles, short-term probabilities lean positive around 55% for the April 6 resolution. As forecast horizon increases, the mean reversion signal weakens, quarterly dividend drops (approx 1.2% every 3 months) partially offset gross drift, and aggregate variance overwhelms the drift edge, forcing expected probabilities closer to 0.50 (in lockstep with direct evaluation instructions on distant forecast horizons). Thus probabilities decay progressively towards 51% for the 1-year mark.
